description_long: This is a utility knife with a retractable segmented blade, commonly known as a box cutter. The body is primarily yellow with black accents, featuring an ergonomic plastic handle designed for a comfortable grip. The blade is metallic, straight, and segmented, allowing for snapping off dull sections to reveal a fresh edge. A black circular wheel on the side serves as a locking mechanism to adjust and secure the blade's length. The handle has a small hole at the rear, likely for attaching a lanyard or hanging the tool. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, and is intended for practical cutting tasks in urban or workshop environments.
description: A yellow and black utility knife with a segmented retractable blade and ergonomic plastic handle, featuring a side locking wheel.
description_view_0: A narrow end-on view showing the slim profile and the tip of the extended blade.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the yellow handle, black segmented blade, and the overall length of the knife.
description_view_2: A reverse end-on view showing the thinness of the blade and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displaying the locking wheel and the full length of the segmented blade.
attribute_handle_design: contoured plastic handle with ergonomic grip and lanyard hole
